User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the DailyPulse application. This entity only includes basic information necessary for internal relationships and does not store authentication credentials, which are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Mood.title: Mood
Mood.description: Defines the predefined mood categories available for user selection in the DailyPulse application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Mood entity.

description.description: A brief, friendly description of the mood.

emoji.description: The emoji character or icon identifier representing the mood for UI display.

MoodGuideResponseTemplate.title: MoodGuideResponseTemplate
MoodGuideResponseTemplate.description: Stores pre-defined supportive messages and practical suggestions associated with a specific mood, allowing for variety and personalization in daily guide responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MoodGuideResponseTemplate entity.

moodId.description: Reference to the Mood this response template is associated with. (Relationship: Mood 1:N MoodGuideResponseTemplate)

message.description: A short, supportive message to be displayed to the user for the associated mood.

suggestion1.description: The first practical suggestion for the user, relevant to the associated mood.

suggestion2.description: The second practical suggestion for the user, relevant to the associated mood (optional).

UserMoodEntry.title: UserMoodEntry
UserMoodEntry.description: Records a user's daily mood check-in, including the selected mood and the specific guide response presented to them, providing a historical log.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserMoodEntry entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this mood entry. (Relationship: User 1:N UserMoodEntry)

moodId.description: Reference to the Mood selected by the user for this entry. (Relationship: Mood 1:N UserMoodEntry)

entryDate.description: The specific date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the user performed the mood check-in. Used to enforce one entry per day.

selectedMessage.description: The specific supportive message presented to the user for this entry, copied from a MoodGuideResponseTemplate to ensure historical accuracy.

selectedSuggestion1.description: The specific first practical suggestion presented to the user for this entry, copied for historical accuracy.

selectedSuggestion2.description: The specific second practical suggestion presented to the user for this entry (if applicable), copied for historical accuracy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this mood entry record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this mood entry record was last updated (e.g., if a user edits their daily mood).

definition.description: Stores core user profile data, indexed by their Firebase Authentication UID. This acts as the root for all user-specific subcollections, ensuring path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains predefined, global mood categories available to all users. This collection is publicly readable and managed by the backend/admin.

params.name: moodId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific mood category.

definition.description: Holds pre-defined supportive messages and practical suggestions associated with different moods. This collection is publicly readable and managed by the backend/admin, allowing for variety in daily guide responses. Includes a 'moodId' field for linking to a specific mood for retrieval, but not for authorization.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific mood guide response template.

definition.description: Records a user's daily mood check-in. The document ID (`entryDate` in YYYY-MM-DD format) is used to enforce the 'one entry per day' rule. Access is restricted to the owning user (`userId` from the path). Includes denormalized 'selectedMessage' and 'selectedSuggestion' fields for historical accuracy.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made this mood entry.

params.name: entryDate
params.description: The specific date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the mood entry, serving as the document ID to enforce uniqueness per day per user.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure robust security rules and efficient data access. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

1.  **User-Owned Data (`/users/{userId}/userMoodEntries/{entryDate}`):** Authorization is achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into the path. This allows Firestore security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path for all read/write operations on a user's mood entries. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to a parent document or to check the `userId` field within the `UserMoodEntry` document itself for authorization, thereby maintaining atomic operations (transactions/batches) and simplifying rule logic. The `entryDate` as the document ID further aids in enforcing the 'one entry per day' rule directly within security rules, enhancing both data integrity and authorization. For example, a create rule can easily verify `request.resource.id == request.time.toDate('YYYY-MM-DD')`.

2.  **Global Data (`/moods/{moodId}`, `/moodGuideResponseTemplates/{templateId}`):** These collections store static, publicly readable data. Since their security posture is uniform (read-only for all authenticated users, write for backend/admin only), no complex authorization logic or denormalization is required, ensuring maximum independence and simplicity.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **Global Collections (`/moods`, `/moodGuideResponseTemplates`):** All documents within these collections share the same security requirements (publicly readable). Therefore, `list` operations are inherently secure as they do not require client-side filtering and the rules permit access to all documents uniformly.

2.  **User-Specific Collection (`/users/{userId}/userMoodEntries`):** The hierarchical path structure ensures that `list` operations are secure. When a user queries their mood history, the query path will include their specific `userId` (e.g., `/users/ABC123/userMoodEntries`). The security rules will then enforce that `userId == request.auth.uid`, effectively pre-filtering the access to only the authenticated user's data. This allows for efficient and secure `list` queries without violating the 'rules are not filters' principle, as the path itself acts as a principal filter enforced by the security rules.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   User-private data (`userMoodEntries`) is strictly segregated under `/users/{userId}/`. 
*   Global, public data (`moods`, `moodGuideResponseTemplates`) resides in top-level collections.

This segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture, leading to simpler, more readable, and less error-prone security rules. The design avoids mixing different access requirements within the same collection.
